description:
You are acting as a junior penetration tester who has just received the raw results of an nmap scan carried out by a senior colleague.  
    All raw data for the target host has already been placed on the machine for you in the following location:

        /home/user/assessment/raw/host1_nmap.txt

    Your job is to create a clean, easily-readable list of the services that are **currently listening** on that host.  
    The senior tester only wants to see the TCP ports whose state is reported as “open”.

    1. Parse /home/user/assessment/raw/host1_nmap.txt and produce a new file called  

           /home/user/assessment/logs/host1_open_ports.log

       (create the /home/user/assessment/logs directory if it does not yet exist).

    2. The content of host1_open_ports.log **must follow this exact format** so that our automated checking tools can validate it:

       • Line 1  → exactly:     # Open Ports Report  
       • Line 2  → exactly:     Host: 127.0.0.1  
       • Line 3+ → one line per open TCP port, sorted in ascending numerical order.  
                    Each line must look like:  
                        <port_number>/tcp - <service_name>

         Example line (do not add the “Example” word):  
             22/tcp - ssh

       • End the file with a single trailing newline (i.e. the file’s last byte is “\n”).

    3. Use only standard GNU/Linux user-land tools already available in a minimal distribution (e.g. grep, awk, cut, sort, sed, mkdir, etc.).  
       The entire task can be completed with **one single compound shell command** (pipelines are fine) plus any mkdir you might need.

    When you have finished, the grader will inspect /home/user/assessment/logs/host1_open_ports.log.  
    If the file exists and its content matches the specification *byte-for-byte*, the task is considered successful.
truth:
<!-- PRE-EXISTING FILE -->
    File: /home/user/assessment/raw/host1_nmap.txt  
    Contents (UNIX newlines):  
    Starting Nmap 7.80 ( https://nmap.org ) at 2023-01-01 00:00 UTC  
    Nmap scan report for localhost (127.0.0.1)  
    Host is up (0.00012s latency).  
    Not shown: 997 closed ports  
    PORT     STATE  SERVICE VERSION  
    22/tcp   open   ssh     OpenSSH 8.2 (protocol 2.0)  
    80/tcp   closed http  
    443/tcp  open   https   Apache httpd 2.4.41 ((Ubuntu))  
    8080/tcp filtered http-proxy  
    MAC Address: AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF (Unknown)  
    Service detection performed. Please report any incorrect results at https://nmap.org/submit/ .  
    Nmap done: 1 IP address (1 host up) scanned in 0.48 seconds  

    <!-- EXPECTED OUTPUT FILE -->
    File that the agent must create: /home/user/assessment/logs/host1_open_ports.log  
    Expected exact content (UNIX newlines, final newline mandatory):  
    # Open Ports Report  
    Host: 127.0.0.1  
    22/tcp - ssh  
    443/tcp - https
name: task_000000_2d1b8199
